rounds.blurb: Orville Wright pilots the first powered flight at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina, while Wilbur runs alongside.
rounds.credit: John T. Daniels / Library of Congress — public domain
rounds.story: The flight lasted twelve seconds and covered 120 feet — shorter than a 747's wingspan. John Daniels, a lifeguard who had never worked a camera before, squeezed the shutter bulb exactly once that morning and captured one of history's most reproduced images.

rounds.blurb: Dorothea Lange's "Migrant Mother": Florence Owens Thompson in a pea pickers' camp in Nipomo, California.
rounds.credit: Dorothea Lange / Farm Security Administration — public domain
rounds.story: Florence Owens Thompson, 32 and a mother of seven, sat in a pea pickers' camp where the crop had frozen. Lange stopped for ten minutes and made six exposures; the last became the defining image of the Great Depression.

rounds.blurb: Pluto in true color, captured by New Horizons during humanity's first flyby of the dwarf planet.
rounds.story: New Horizons flew nine and a half years and three billion miles for a flyby lasting hours, revealing a heart-shaped nitrogen glacier on a world demoted from planethood mid-journey. Discoverer Clyde Tombaugh's ashes rode aboard.

rounds.blurb: A carpooling appeal near Vancouver, Washington, as the first oil crisis dragged on.
rounds.credit: David Falconer / EPA Documerica, National Archives — public domain
rounds.story: OPEC's embargo quadrupled oil prices in months; America answered with odd-even gas rationing, a 55 mph national speed limit, and signs like this one. The era of the cheap-gas land yacht never came back.

rounds.blurb: Occupy Wall Street gathers near Zuccotti Park on the movement's first day.
rounds.story: A few hundred people in a granite plaza grew into encampments in hundreds of cities and put 'the 1%' into the political vocabulary. The Zuccotti Park camp lasted 59 days before police cleared it.
